blocks.text: Calamus heatubunii has an area of occupancy (AOO) and extent of occurrence (EOO) that fall below the respective thresholds for the Endangered category under Criterion B. There are also only 4 known locations and a projected continuing decline in the area and extent of its habitat as a result of deforestation caused by the urban expansion of Sorong and the active palm oil concessions, and also a projected decline in the AOO, EOO and number of locations. This species is therefore listed as Endangered B1ab(i,ii,iii,iv)+2ab(i,ii,iii,iv). On Waigeo the only known location is in a palm oil concession that is not yet active, but the species would not be able to survive in this area if it became active in the future.
blocks.text_rich: Calamus heatubunii has an area of occupancy (AOO) and extent of occurrence (EOO) that fall below the respective thresholds for the Endangered category under Criterion B. There are also only 4 known locations and a projected continuing decline in the area and extent of its habitat as a result of deforestation caused by the urban expansion of Sorong and the active palm oil concessions, and also a projected decline in the AOO, EOO and number of locations. This species is therefore listed as Endangered B1ab(i,ii,iii,iv)+2ab(i,ii,iii,iv). On Waigeo the only known location is in a palm oil concession that is not yet active, but the species would not be able to survive in this area if it became active in the future.

blocks.text: Calamus heatubunii has been recorded in the far west of mainland New Guinea from several localities around Sorong in West Papua province, and from the island of Waigeo in the Raja Ampat Islands. The known elevation range as recorded from herbarium specimens is between 45 and 180 m asl.
blocks.text_rich: Calamus heatubunii has been recorded in the far west of mainland New Guinea from several localities around Sorong in West Papua province, and from the island of Waigeo in the Raja Ampat Islands. The known elevation range as recorded from herbarium specimens is between 45 and 180 m asl.

blocks.text: Calamus heatubunii has been recorded from lowland forest, including secondary, hill and swamp forest (Baker and Dransfield 2017).

blocks.text: The majority of the known herbarium collections were made close to Sorong from 1994 to 2013, with a single record from Waigeo in 1997. The Sorong lowlands are undergoing forest loss as a result of the expansion of the city of Sorong and the active palm oil concession PT Henrison Inti Persada (Global Forest Watch 2022), which is visible on satellite photos. Most of Waigeo is designated as a Nature Reserve, however the single record of this species from Waigeo is from close to Wagaliom, inside the oil palm concession PT. Irmasulindo 1. This concession does not appear to be active yet from satellite photos. Most of the collections in Baker and Dransfield (2017) are from villages and secondary forest, so the species appears to survive in disturbed forest.

Most of Waigeo is designated as a Nature Reserve, however the single record of this species from Waigeo is from close to Wagaliom, inside the oil palm concession PT. Irmasulindo 1. This concession does not appear to be active yet from satellite photos. Most of the collections in Baker and Dransfield (2017) are from villages and secondary forest, so the species appears to survive in disturbed forest.

blocks.text: Calamus heatubunii is not found in any protected areas. Much of Waigeo island is a protected area, but the lowlands where this palm is most likely to occur are not protected. The distribution of this palm on Waigeo is unknown, so it may occur in the Waigeo Nature Reserve. Calamus heatubunii is not found in any ex situ collections (BGCI 2022).

rows: Occur in at least one PA
One record from Waigeo outside of the protected area, but as this covers most of the island so this species may occur inside the boundary.
